lines.speaker: Captain
lines.text: Wind at our back, coin in the chest. Rest if you must, traveler. Tarshish is a long way west.

lines.speaker: Captain
lines.text: This is no common squall. Lash the cargo before the sea tears us open.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: The side cleats must hold fast. Leave the center loose so the deck can breathe with the swell.

lines.speaker: Captain
lines.text: Good. The cargo will ride. Now answer plainly: who has brought this storm upon us?

lines.speaker: Jonah
lines.text: I am a Hebrew, and I fear the LORD, the God of heaven, who made the sea and the dry land.

lines.speaker: Jonah
lines.text: I am fleeing from before His face. Lift me up and cast me into the sea, and then it will quiet down for you.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The sailors rowed hard to return to land, but the sea grew more tempestuous against them.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: At last they lifted Jonah and hurled him into the sea, and the sea ceased from its raging.

lines.speaker: Helm
lines.text: The wheel bucks against the storm like a living thing.
